subject: DevOps
title: Artifacts and Registries
description: A quick, practical guide to Artifacts and Registries.

hook.text: The build stage produced a binary. Where does it go? You can't just leave it on the build server.

buildup.text: An artifact registry stores build outputs: container images, JAR files, npm packages, binaries.

discovery.text: The deploy stage pulls the exact artifact the build stage produced. Same bits, no rebuild, no drift.

twist.text: Registries also enable rollback. Old versions stay stored. Rolling back means deploying an older artifact.

climax.text: Docker Hub, GitHub Packages, and Artifactory are popular registries. Every serious pipeline uses one.

punchline.text: Build once, store the artifact, deploy it anywhere. No rebuilds.

quiz.question: Why should pipelines deploy stored artifacts instead of rebuilding?
options: To guarantee the exact same bits are deployed every time
To save disk space on the build server
Because source code is deleted after building
